What are we learning?

This unit covers four essential grammar topics in English
🎒 School Supplies
🔤 Indefinite Article
📝 Plurals
👉 Demonstrative Pronouns
🎒

School Supplies

Questions & Answers about school objects
Q What is that?
A It is a pen.
Q What are those?
A They are dictionaries.
Q Is this a sharpener?
A Yes, it is. It is a sharpener.
Q Are these markers?
A No, they aren't. They are pencils.
📌 This is the classroom.
Q What is that?
A It is the wastebasket.
Q What are these?
A They are the windows.
Q Is this the floor?
A Yes, it is. It is the floor.
Q Are those the curtains?
A No, they aren't. They are the blinds.
🔤

Indefinite Article

"a" and "an" — un / una
Key Concept

El artículo indefinido en Inglés es "a" y "an" (un o una) y se usa solo en singular.

a man / un hombre  ·  a ruler / una regla  ·  an apple / una manzana

🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Nótese que la misma forma sirve tanto para el masculino como para el femenino:
a boy / un muchacho  ·  a girl / una muchacha
A
Se utiliza "a" antes de palabras que empiezan con sonido consonante:
a classroom, a book, a pencil case, a desk.
AN
Se utiliza "an" antes de palabras que comienzan con sonido vocal:
an orange, an eraser, an apple, an umbrella, an insect.
⚠️ Excepciones
La letra "u" tiene dos sonidos:
1) Cuando suena como una /o/, usa "an": an umbrella, an umpire, an uncle.
2) Cuando suena como /iu/ usa "a": a university, a uniform, a unicorn.
La letra "h" tiene igual dualidad:
1) Cuando suena como una "j" usa "a": a hotel, a house, a hammer.
2) Cuando no tiene sonido (es muda), usa "an": an heir, an hour, an honor.
📝

Plurals

Regular & Irregular plurals
El plural se refiere a dos o más personas, animales o cosas. Y se clasifican en regular e irregular.
R
Regular Plurals
1
La mayoría de las palabras se les agrega una "s" al singular:
an eraser → erasers
a book → books
a schoolbag → schoolbags
2
Si la palabra termina con "sh", "ch", "s", "ss", "x", "z", se le añade "es"
Ejemplos: brush → brushes, watch → watches, box → boxes, bus → buses
3
Algunas palabras que terminan con "o" se le añade "es":
Ejemplos: tomato → tomatoes, potato → potatoes, hero → heroes
4
Si la palabra termina con vocal + "y", también se le añade "s":
Ejemplos: key → keys, boy → boys, day → days, toy → toys
5
Si la palabra termina con consonante + "y", se cambia la "y" por una "i" y se le añade "es":
Ejemplos: city → cities, baby → babies, family → families, country → countries
I
Irregular Plurals
1) Los que cambian:
man → men
woman → women
child → children
foot → feet
tooth → teeth
mouse → mice
goose → geese
ox → oxen
2) Los que quedan igual:
sheep → sheep
fish → fish
deer → deer
aircraft → aircraft
species → species
3) Las palabras que terminan en "f" o "fe", cambian por "ves":
leaf → leaves
wife → wives
knife → knives
wolf → wolves
half → halves
shelf → shelves
👉

Demonstrative Pronouns

This / That / These / Those
📍 Near — Cerca
This / These
This is a book. (singular)
These are books. (plural)
🌐 Far — Lejos
That / Those
That is a pen. (singular)
Those are pens. (plural)
💬 Practice — Questions & Answers
Q What is that?
A It is a pen.
Q What are those?
A They are dictionaries.
Q Is this a sharpener?
A Yes, it is. It is a sharpener.
Q Are these markers?
A No, they aren't. They are pencils.
